EnableAutomation.Name: Enable Combat Automation
EnableAutomation.Hint: Automatically track combat rounds, turns, and apply combat effects

AutoConditionRecovery.Name: Automatic Condition Recovery
AutoConditionRecovery.Hint: Prompt for condition track recovery checks at the start of each combatant's turn

AutoDamageThreshold.Name: Automatic Damage Threshold
AutoDamageThreshold.Hint: Automatically move characters down the condition track when damage exceeds their damage threshold

WeaponRangeMultiplier.Name: Weapon Range Multiplier
WeaponRangeMultiplier.Hint: Multiply all weapon ranges by this factor (useful for different scale grids)

ForcePointBonus.Name: Force Point Reroll Bonus
ForcePointBonus.Hint: Bonus die size when spending Force Points to reroll (d6 = +1d6)

StoreMarkup.Name: Store Price Markup
StoreMarkup.Hint: Percentage markup for items purchased from the store (can be negative for discounts)

StoreDiscount.Name: Store Sell Discount
StoreDiscount.Hint: Percentage of original price when selling items to the store

CharacterCreation.Name: Character Creation Rules
CharacterCreation.Label: Configure
CharacterCreation.Hint: Configure ability score generation, HP, and character creation options

Advancement.Name: Advancement Rules
Advancement.Label: Configure
Advancement.Hint: Configure level-up, talents, feats, and progression options

Combat.Name: Combat Rules
Combat.Label: Configure
Combat.Hint: Configure combat, damage, death, and condition track rules

Force.Name: Force & Destiny Rules
Force.Label: Configure
Force.Hint: Configure Force Points, Dark Side, and Force power rules

Presets.Name: Houserule Presets
Presets.Label: Manage Presets
Presets.Hint: Apply preset houserule configurations or import/export settings

SecondWindImproved.Name: Improved Second Wind
SecondWindImproved.Hint: Second Wind also improves condition track by one step

TalentEveryLevel.Name: Talent Every Level
TalentEveryLevel.Hint: Characters gain a talent at every level instead of every odd level

CrossClassSkills.Name: Cross-Class Skill Training
CrossClassSkills.Hint: Allow training non-class skills for 2 skill slots

SkillFocusRestriction.Name: Skill Focus Restrictions
SkillFocusRestriction.Hint: Restrict Skill Focus (Use the Force) to prevent early game dominance

ArmoredDefenseForAll.Name: Armored Defense for All
ArmoredDefenseForAll.Hint: All characters can use armor bonus to Reflex Defense

WeaponRanges.Name: Weapon Range Modifier
WeaponRanges.Hint: Multiply all weapon ranges by this factor

AthleticsConsolidation.Name: Athletics Skill
AthleticsConsolidation.Hint: Merge Climb, Jump, and Swim into single Athletics skill

DiagonalMovement.Name: Diagonal Movement
DiagonalMovement.Hint: How to count diagonal movement on grid

ConditionTrackCap.Name: Condition Track Damage Cap
ConditionTrackCap.Hint: Maximum condition track steps from single attack

DarkSideTemptation.Name: Dark Side Temptation
DarkSideTemptation.Hint: Spend Force Point to temporarily gain Dark Side powers

DailyForcePoints.Name: Daily Force Points
DailyForcePoints.Hint: Use daily Force Points instead of per-level (1-5th: 1 FP, 6-10th: 2 FP, 11-15th: 3 FP, 16+: 4 FP per day)

DarkSideTemptationMechanic.Name: Dark Side Temptation Mechanic
DarkSideTemptationMechanic.Hint: Allow characters to call upon the Dark Side when spending Force Points (requires Dark Side Score ≤ half Wisdom, adds +1d6, increases Dark Side Score by 1)

ResetResourcesOnCombat.Name: Reset Resources on Combat Start
ResetResourcesOnCombat.Hint: Automatically reset temporary resources (like Second Wind) when combat begins

WelcomeShown.Name: Welcome Message Shown
WelcomeShown.Hint: Tracks if the welcome message has been displayed

DevMode.Name: Developer Mode
DevMode.Hint: Enable detailed error logging and stack traces for debugging

SpendDialog.Title: Spend Force Point
SpendDialog.Question: Spend a Force Point for {reason}?
SpendDialog.RollInfo: You will roll {die} and add the {method} to your roll.
SpendDialog.CallDarkSide: Call upon the Dark Side (+1d6, increases Dark Side Score by 1)

Chat.Title: Force Point Spent
Chat.AvoidDeath: {name} spends a Force Point to avoid death and falls unconscious!
Chat.AlreadyZero: Your Dark Side Score is already 0.

Welcome.Title: SWSE Enhanced System Loaded!
Welcome.Summary: Check the Summary tab for your combat dashboard

Condition.RecoverySuccess: {name} recovers one step on the condition track!

Damage.CannotRoll: Cannot roll damage for this item.

Items.NotOwnedByActor: This item is not owned by an actor.

ConditionRecovery.Title: Condition Recovery
ConditionRecovery.Content:
{name} can attempt condition recovery.

Current position: {current}/5

Make a DC 10 Endurance check?

Healing.DroidNote: Droids can only be repaired with the Mechanics skill.

RequireGMApproval.Name: Require GM Approval for Custom Droids
RequireGMApproval.Hint: When enabled, custom droids must be approved by a GM before credits are deducted. When disabled, custom droids are immediately finalized.

DisableCallouts.Name: Disable Guided Callouts
DisableCallouts.Hint: Turn off first-use hint callouts that appear when opening features for the first time

DisableTour.Name: Disable First-Launch Tour
DisableTour.Hint: Turn off the feature overview that appears on first launch

DisableTooltips.Name: Disable Discovery Tooltips
DisableTooltips.Hint: Turn off contextual tooltips on UI elements

HitPoints.Title: Hit Points
HitPoints.Body: Hit Points measure how long you can stay in the fight. They reflect both actual injury tolerance and combat skill such as dodging, deflecting, and turning solid hits into less serious harm.

DamageThreshold.Title: Damage Threshold
DamageThreshold.Body: Damage Threshold measures how much damage a single attack must inflict to seriously impair you. Even if you still have Hit Points left, a sufficiently powerful hit can reduce your effectiveness immediately.

ForcePoints.Title: Force Points
ForcePoints.Body: Force Points represent a character's ability to draw on unusual help, whether understood as destiny, luck, or the Force moving through living beings. They are a limited resource used to improve important actions.

DestinyPoints.Title: Destiny Points
DestinyPoints.Body: Rare points that let you automatically succeed on a roll or trigger special destiny effects.

ConditionTrack.Title: Condition Track
ConditionTrack.Body: Tracks penalties from taking heavy hits. Each step down applies cumulative -1 to all rolls and defenses.

BaseAttackBonus.Title: Base Attack Bonus
BaseAttackBonus.Body: Added to attack rolls. Determined by class and level.

Grapple.Title: Grapple
Grapple.Body: Used for grab and grapple checks. Based on BAB and size modifier.

Initiative.Title: Initiative
Initiative.Body: Initiative determines how fast you react when action starts. A higher result usually means acting earlier in the round.

ReflexDefense.Title: Reflex Defense
ReflexDefense.Body: Targeted by most attacks. Based on class, level, Dexterity or armor bonus.

FortitudeDefense.Title: Fortitude Defense
FortitudeDefense.Body: Resists physical effects like poison and stun. Based on class, level, and Constitution.

WillDefense.Title: Will Defense
WillDefense.Body: Resists mental effects and Force powers. Based on class, level, and Wisdom.

FlatFooted.Title: Flat-Footed
FlatFooted.Body: Defense when caught off guard. Does not include Dexterity or dodge bonuses.

AbilityScore.Title: Ability Score
AbilityScore.Body: The raw score. The modifier (score minus 10, divided by 2) is what applies to rolls.

AbilityModifier.Title: Ability Modifier
AbilityModifier.Body: Derived from the ability score. Added to relevant rolls, skills, and defenses.

SkillTrained.Title: Trained Skill
SkillTrained.Body: Trained skills receive a +5 bonus and may unlock special uses not available untrained.

SkillRoll.Title: Skill Check
SkillRoll.Body: Click to roll this skill. The roll adds your modifier and training bonus.

PassiveTalent.Title: Passive Talent
PassiveTalent.Body: Always active. No action required to benefit from this talent.

ActiveTalent.Title: Active Talent
ActiveTalent.Body: Requires an action to use. Check the talent description for action type.

UsageLimit.Title: Limited Use
UsageLimit.Body: This ability can only be used a set number of times per encounter or per day.

WeaponAttack.Title: Attack Roll
WeaponAttack.Body: Click to roll an attack with this weapon against a target's Reflex Defense.

WeaponDamage.Title: Damage Roll
WeaponDamage.Body: Click to roll damage for this weapon after a successful attack.

ArmorPenalty.Title: Armor Penalty
ArmorPenalty.Body: This armor applies a penalty to Reflex Defense beyond the armor bonus and may restrict Dexterity.

ActionPalette.Title: Action Palette
ActionPalette.Body: Quick access to common combat and narrative actions for your character.

PaletteMode.Title: Palette Mode
PaletteMode.Body: Switches between combat actions and narrative/exploration actions.

ActionDisabled.Title: Action Unavailable
ActionDisabled.Body: This action cannot be used right now. Check prerequisites and available resources.

ChargenNarrative.Title: Narrative Mode
ChargenNarrative.Body: Build your character through guided story choices instead of manual stat selection.

ChargenMentor.Title: Mentor Selection
ChargenMentor.Body: Choose a mentor whose guidance shapes your character creation experience.

ChargenRollMethod.Title: Ability Score Method
ChargenRollMethod.Body: Select how to generate ability scores: roll dice, point buy, or standard array.

ChargenSpecies.Title: Species
ChargenSpecies.Body: Your species determines base traits, ability adjustments, and available features.

ChargenClass.Title: Class
ChargenClass.Body: Your class determines hit points, defenses, trained skills, and talent access.

ChargenLockin.Title: Finalize Character
ChargenLockin.Body: Confirms your choices. Most selections can still be adjusted after creation.

MentorDialogue.Title: Mentor Guidance
MentorDialogue.Body: Your mentor offers context and suggestions. This is advisory, not mandatory.

AurebeshTranslation.Title: Aurebesh Translation
AurebeshTranslation.Body: Text appears in Aurebesh first, then translates. Click to skip the effect.

TacticalOverlay.Title: Tactical Overlay
TacticalOverlay.Body: Shows range bands, threat areas, and tactical information on the map.

GMSuggestion.Title: GM Suggestion
GMSuggestion.Body: Advisory insight from the suggestion engine. Not applied automatically.

GMPaletteMode.Title: GM Mode
GMPaletteMode.Body: Switches the palette to GM tools: NPC spawning, healing, condition management.

Strength.Title: Strength
Strength.Body: Strength reflects how much force a character can apply physically. It is especially important in direct physical action and close-quarters power-based tasks.

Dexterity.Title: Dexterity
Dexterity.Body: Dexterity reflects fine control, reaction speed, and physical precision. It is especially valuable for ranged combat, evasive movement, and vehicle handling.

Constitution.Title: Constitution
Constitution.Body: Constitution reflects how well a character withstands injury, fatigue, and bodily strain. It is a core survivability stat because it contributes to staying power.

Intelligence.Title: Intelligence
Intelligence.Body: Intelligence reflects how well a character understands systems, solves problems, and develops trained expertise. It is especially useful for characters who rely on broad technical or academic skill.

Wisdom.Title: Wisdom
Wisdom.Body: Wisdom reflects perception, practical insight, and connection to surroundings. It is about awareness and sound judgment more than abstract analysis.

Charisma.Title: Charisma
Charisma.Body: Charisma reflects leadership, magnetism, confidence, and the ability to move others. It is not just appearance; it is the strength of a character's personal presence.

Acrobatics.Title: Acrobatics
Acrobatics.Body: Acrobatics covers balance, tumbling, safe falls, escaping physical holds, and agile movement. Use it when precision body control matters more than raw strength.

Climb.Title: Climb
Climb.Body: Climb is used to ascend difficult surfaces such as rock faces, buildings, ladders, and machinery. It measures controlled upward movement through strength and grip.

Deception.Title: Deception
Deception.Body: Deception is used to make falsehoods seem believable, create confusion, conceal intent, or present yourself as someone else. It covers bluffing, fast-talking, disguise, and forged evidence.

Endurance.Title: Endurance
Endurance.Body: Endurance measures stamina and resilience under stress. Use it when a character must keep going through fatigue, hardship, or other physical extremes.

GatherInformation.Title: Gather Information
GatherInformation.Body: Gather Information is used to ask around, work social networks, and uncover leads. It helps locate gossip, public talk, quiet secrets, and useful connections.

Jump.Title: Jump
Jump.Body: Jump is used for clearing pits, vaulting barriers, and making controlled leaps. It reflects explosive strength and coordinated movement.

Knowledge.Title: Knowledge
Knowledge.Body: Knowledge represents academic, professional, or technical learning. Each field covers a different body of lore, so success depends on the subject being relevant to your training.

Mechanics.Title: Mechanics
Mechanics.Body: Mechanics is used to interact directly with machinery and technical hardware. It covers bypassing security, setting or disarming explosives, and repairing or improving damaged equipment and droids.

Perception.Title: Perception
Perception.Body: Perception is used to detect threats, spot concealed creatures or objects, and stay aware of the environment. It reflects alertness and sensory awareness.

Persuasion.Title: Persuasion
Persuasion.Body: Persuasion is used to win cooperation, shape attitudes, and steer social outcomes. It can be subtle and diplomatic or forceful and intimidating.

Pilot.Title: Pilot
Pilot.Body: Pilot governs advanced vehicle handling. Routine travel may not require a check, but evasive action, stunts, and dangerous control do.

Ride.Title: Ride
Ride.Body: Ride is used to handle creatures used as mounts, especially in motion or under stress. It reflects balance, control, and mounted coordination.

Stealth.Title: Stealth
Stealth.Body: Stealth is used to avoid detection through silence, concealment, and careful movement. It also supports hidden positioning, ambushes, sniping from cover, and sleight of hand.

Survival.Title: Survival
Survival.Body: Survival helps characters navigate harsh environments, track signs of danger, forage, hunt, and identify natural hazards. It measures practical fieldcraft.

Swim.Title: Swim
Swim.Body: Swim is used to cross water, dive, stay afloat, and maneuver in difficult aquatic conditions. It represents controlled movement in water.

TreatInjury.Title: Treat Injury
TreatInjury.Body: Treat Injury covers battlefield aid, medical care, and recovery support. Use it to keep allies alive, improve healing, and respond to harmful conditions.

UseComputer.Title: Use Computer
UseComputer.Body: Use Computer is used to operate secured systems, bypass digital protections, and interact with complex computer-controlled technology. It governs technical access in electronic environments.

UseTheForce.Title: Use the Force
UseTheForce.Body: Use the Force represents active connection to the Force in play. It supports unusual perception, recovery, and other force-driven acts, and it normally requires Force Sensitivity to be trained.

DarkSideScore.Title: Dark Side Score
DarkSideScore.Body: Dark Side Score tracks moral and spiritual corruption through evil acts. A higher score reflects greater influence from the dark side and may carry serious consequences.

CatchSecondWind.Title: Catch a Second Wind
CatchSecondWind.Body: Catch a Second Wind lets a heroic character rally after being seriously hurt. It is only available when reduced to half hit points or less, and it can be used once per day.

actionPaletteFirstOpen.Title: Action Palette
actionPaletteFirstOpen.Body: This is your quick-access action menu. It adapts to combat and narrative contexts. You can customize it to fit your playstyle.

tacticalOverlayFirstOpen.Title: Tactical Overlay
tacticalOverlayFirstOpen.Body: Toggle map overlays showing range bands, threat zones, and positioning data. Each layer can be toggled independently.

mentorTranslationFirst.Title: Mentor Dialogue
mentorTranslationFirst.Body: Mentor text appears with an Aurebesh translation effect. Click anywhere on the text to skip the animation and read immediately.

gmSuggestionPanelFirst.Title: GM Suggestions
gmSuggestionPanelFirst.Body: These are advisory insights based on the current encounter. Nothing is applied automatically. Use them as you see fit.

chargenNarrativeFirst.Title: Character Creation Modes
chargenNarrativeFirst.Body: You can build your character through narrative choices or direct mechanical selection. Both paths produce a fully valid character.

Player.Title: Welcome to Star Wars Saga Edition
Player.Subtitle: Here are a few things to help you get started. All features are optional.
Player.Item1: Character creation supports both narrative and mechanical modes
Player.Item2: The Action Palette provides quick access to combat and narrative actions
Player.Item3: Mentors can guide your choices during character creation
Player.Item4: Hover over any highlighted UI element for a quick explanation
Player.Item5: All guided hints can be turned off in your client settings

GM.Title: Welcome to Star Wars Saga Edition (GM Tools)
GM.Subtitle: Your GM toolkit includes several optional systems. Nothing runs unless you enable it.
GM.Item1: The Tactical Overlay adds range and threat visualization to scenes
GM.Item2: GM Suggestions offer advisory encounter insights, never automatic
GM.Item3: Scene tools and NPC management are available from the sidebar and palette
GM.Item4: All automation (damage threshold, condition recovery) can be toggled in settings
GM.Item5: Discovery hints can be reset or disabled from system settings

Confirm.AllRequiredComplete: All required build elements are complete.
Confirm.ResolveItems: Resolve these items to proceed to finalization.
Confirm.ReviewOrFinalize: Click on any section above to review details, or proceed to finalize your character.

Details.RepeatableBody: You can select this feat multiple times if you have additional feat picks.
Details.WhySuggestedBody: This feat synergizes well with your build and class.

Empty.DisplayRulesDescription: No feats meet this slot's current display rules.
Empty.NoFeatsInViewDescription: Try another category, clear search, or toggle Show All to inspect locked options.
Empty.NoLegalStartingFeatsDescription: No feat from this class's starting-feat pool currently meets your prerequisites.
Empty.NoSearchMatches: No feats match “{query}” in the feat catalog.
Empty.SafeModeBrowserBody: This feat step will not block character creation while the compendium is unavailable.
Empty.SafeModeContinueBody: Continue is allowed so chargen does not soft-lock.
Empty.SearchHint: Try a shorter name, prerequisite, or tag.
Empty.ShowAllTip: Enable "Show All" to inspect blocked feat prerequisites.

Footer.CatalogUnavailableSafeToSkip: {type} Feat: Catalog unavailable — safe to skip
Footer.NoLegalOptionsSafeToSkip: {type} Feat: No legal options — safe to skip

Messages.CatalogHydrationFailed: The feat catalog did not hydrate from the Foundry compendium. This step is safe to skip, but feat choices will not be available until the feats pack registers.
Messages.CatalogUnavailableSkippable: The feat catalog is unavailable because the feats compendium did not register. The step remains skippable so character creation can continue.
Messages.CatalogUnavailableWarning: Feat catalog unavailable: no feats were loaded from the compendium. You can continue past this step.
Messages.ChoiceNotLegal: That feat choice is not currently legal.
Messages.GeneralFeatLimit: You have already selected {count} general feats. Remove one before choosing another.
Messages.NotCurrentlyAvailable: That feat is not currently available.
Messages.PrereqsNotMetForChoice: Feat prerequisites are not met for that selected choice.
Messages.ReturnToSkillsQuestion: Would you like to return to Skill Selection now so you can rearrange your trained skills?
Messages.SkillSelectionNotBehindCurrent: Skill Selection is not behind the current step in this progression flow.
Messages.SkillsStepUnavailable: The Skills step is not currently available.
Messages.UseTheForceNowClassSkill: is now a class skill for this character.
Messages.WeaponProficiencyChosenGroup: Weapon Proficiency with chosen weapon group or exotic weapon

Work.SearchResultsHint: Only feats related to your search are shown here.
Work.SelectInspectHint: Select a feat to inspect it in the detail rail.
Work.ToggleShowAllTitle: Toggle between legal selectable feats and the full hydrated feat browser

Mentor.Chargen: Choose feats that strengthen your abilities and define your playstyle. Some feats are better for your build than others.
Mentor.DroidChargen: Choose feats that reinforce your chassis role package and complement your installed systems.
Mentor.LevelUp: As you gain experience, you may learn new techniques and abilities. Choose feats that enhance your path.

Shell.TrainingMatrixSubtitle: Mentor-guided progression pipeline · concept shell restoration pass

Actions.CenterTitle: Refocus the selected or inspected node
Actions.FitTitle: Fit the whole tree in the map panel

Details.RootNode: Root node — no prerequisite talent chain.
Details.NoRulesText: No rules text has been defined for this talent yet.
Details.PartOfTree: {talent} is part of the {tree} talent tree.

Empty.NoTalentsMatched: No talents matched your current filters in this tree.
Empty.NoTreesAvailable: No talent trees available in your current context.

Stage.ClassDisciplineHint: Select a class-specific discipline. Each card opens a tactical holomap of legal, known, and locked talents.
Stage.HeroicDisciplineHint: Select a heroic discipline. Each card opens a tactical holomap of legal, known, and locked talents.
Stage.MapInstructions: Drag the map to move. Scroll to zoom. Legal nodes glow when they can be activated.
Stage.TreeHolomapInstructions: Drag to move · Scroll to zoom · Hover to reveal prerequisite flow · Double-click a legal node to activate.

Tree.FallbackSummary: {tree} talent tree. Open the holomap to inspect legal talents and prerequisite paths.
Tree.StatusAvailable: This discipline is available. Opening the holomap will reveal legal activations and locked future branches.
Tree.StatusMentorStrongFit: Your mentor currently sees this tree as a strong fit. {talent} is the cleanest legal next pick from this tree.
Tree.StatusMomentum: You already have momentum in this discipline. Opening the holomap will show the next legal branches.

Mentor.Chargen: Talents define your path. Choose a discipline that resonates with your vision for this character.
Mentor.Default: Select a talent that defines you.
Mentor.LevelUp: Your talents grow with experience. Choose a new discipline to specialize further in your abilities.

Messages.ChoiceNotLegal: That talent choice is not currently legal.
Messages.PrereqsNotMetForChoice: Talent prerequisites are not met for that selected choice.

Recommendation.NoLegalPicksReason: This tree has no selectable talents for the current slot, so it is not considered by the suggestion engine.
Recommendation.NoSelectableTalentsLegalNow: No selectable talents are legal right now.
Recommendation.ReasonAbility: {name} leans into one of your stronger ability scores.
Recommendation.ReasonChain: {name} continues a prerequisite chain you have already started.
Recommendation.ReasonClass: {name} fits the class path you are currently pursuing.
Recommendation.ReasonLegalNow: {name} is legal now and fits the current talent slot.
Recommendation.ReasonMeta: {name} has a known synergy with your current build signals.
Recommendation.ReasonPrestige: {name} supports a longer prestige or advanced path.
Recommendation.ReasonSkill: {name} uses skills your character is already developing.
Recommendation.ReasonSpecies: {name} lines up with your species or heritage signals.
Recommendation.ReasonWishlist: {name} moves you toward something already marked as desirable for this build.
Recommendation.StrongestLegalPick: {talent} is the strongest legal pick currently visible in this tree.
Recommendation.TreeLegalCount: {tree} has {count} legal talent(s) available for this slot.
